Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine in both serum and blood, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, further supporting possible renal pathology (e.g., nephrotic syndrome or chronic kidney disease). Blood glucose is normal, but HbA1c is low, possibly due to anemia or recent changes in glycemic control. Lipid profile shows mildly elevated total and LDL cholesterol. Recommend nephrology evaluation and further assessment of renal function and underlying causes.
